'''David Fusitu'a''' (born 16 October 1994) is a professional [[rugby league]] footballer who last played as a {{rlp|wg|ce}} and {{rlp|fb}} for [[Leeds Rhinos]] in [[Super League]] and for both [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tonga]] and [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] at international level.

==Early life==
Fusitu'a was born in [[Auckland, New Zealand]].{{cn|date=July 2025}}

Fusitu'a attended [[Kelston Boys' High School]] and played junior football for the [[Marist Saints]].<ref name=bio>{{cite web|url=http://www.warriors.co.nz/team/profiles/david-fusitua.html|title=David Fusitua|access-date=15 March 2014|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140320095017/http://www.warriors.co.nz/team/profiles/david-fusitua.html|archive-date=20 March 2014 }}</ref>

==Playing career==
Fusitu'a was signed by the [[New Zealand Warriors]] and played for the Junior Warriors in 2012 as a 17-year-old. In 2013 he played in the side that lost the [[2013 NRL Under-20s season|Holden Cup]] Grand Final and also spent time with the [[Auckland Vulcans]] in the [[NSW Cup]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.voxy.co.nz/sport/lousi-and-fusitua-out-todays-vulcans-game/5/162545|title=Lousi and Fusitua out for today's Vulcans' game|website=Voxy.co.nz|date=2013-07-27|access-date=2016-02-04}}</ref> Fusitu'a played for New Zealand under-18s in 2011 and the [[Junior Kiwis]] in 2012 and 2013.<ref name=bio/><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.nzrl.co.nz/nz-resident-18s/game-two.aspx |title=NZ Residents 18s: Game Two |publisher=NZRL |archive-url=https://archive.today/20140315034224/http://www.nzrl.co.nz/nz-resident-18s/game-two.aspx |archive-date=15 March 2014}}</ref> Fusitu'a was named in the train on squad for [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tonga]] for the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup|2013 World Cup]] but was not selected in the final squad.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rlwc2013.com/rugby-league-world-news/article/1118/tonga-name-star-studded-initial-train-on|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130927081215/http://www.rlwc2013.com/rugby-league-world-news/article/1118/tonga-name-star-studded-initial-train-on|url-status=usurped|archive-date=27 September 2013|title=Rugby League World Cup 2013|website=Rlwc2013.com|access-date=2016-02-04}}</ref>

===2014===
In February 2014, Fusitu'a was selected for the Warriors inaugural [[2014 Auckland Nines]] squad.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/squads-auckland-nines/|title=Squads For Auckland Nines|publisher=Rugby League Week|date=2014-02-14|access-date=2016-02-04|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160115214258/http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/squads-auckland-nines/|archive-date=2016-01-15}}</ref> In Round 2 of the [[2014 NRL season|2014 NRL]] The best of, Fusitu'a made his first grade debut for the [[New Zealand Warriors]] on the wing, replacing the injured [[Manu Vatuvei]] in the Warriors 31–12 loss at [[Eden Park]].<ref>{{cite web|author=David Long|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/warriors-eden-park-hoodoo-continues-as-dragons-win-3112-20140315-34u1z.html|title=Warriors' Eden Park hoodoo continues as Dragons win 31-12|website=Smh.com.au|date=2016-01-27|access-date=2016-02-04}}</ref> In Round 8 in the ANZAC Day match against the [[Melbourne Storm season 2012|Meplbourne Storm]] at [[AAMI Park]], he scored his first first grade try in the Warriors 16–10 win<ref>{{cite web|author=Roy Ward|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-match-report/new-zealand-warriors-sink-melbourne-storm-before-record-crowd-on-anzac-day-20140425-zqzqo.html|title=New Zealand Warriors sink Melbourne Storm before record crowd on Anzac Day|website=Smh.com.au|date=2014-04-25|access-date=2016-02-04}}</ref> and he finished off his debut year in the NRL with him playing in 12 matches and scoring 7 tries for the Warriors in the [[2014 NRL season]]. On 9 September 2014, Fusitu'a was selected for the [[New Zealand national rugby league team]] [[2014 Rugby League Four Nations]] train on squad.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/kiwis-nations-train-on-squad/|title=Kiwis Name Four Nations Train-On Squad|publisher=Rugby League Week|date=2014-09-09|access-date=2016-02-04|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150717013433/http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/kiwis-nations-train-on-squad/|archive-date=2015-07-17}}</ref> On 12 December 2014, Fusitu'a re-signed with the [[New Zealand Warriors]] on a 2-year contract, keeping him at the club to the end of the 2017 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/warriors-re-sign-high-flying-fusitua/tabid/10874/newsid/83255/default.aspx|title=Warriors re-sign high-flying Fusitu'a|website=NRL.com|date=2014-12-12|access-date=2016-02-04}}</ref>

===2015===
Fusitu’a played in 3 matches and scored 2 tries for the Warriors in an injury-riddled [[2015 NRL season]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nzherald.co.nz/sport/news/article.cfm?c_id=4&objectid=11484577|title=NRL: Fusitua adds to Warriors injury woes - Sport - NZ Herald News|website=[[The New Zealand Herald]]|date=2015-07-21|access-date=2016-02-04}}</ref>

===2016===
In February, Fusitu'a played for the Warriors in the [[2016 NRL Auckland Nines]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/warriors-name-2016-nines-squad/|title=Warriors name 2016 Nines squad|publisher=Rugby League Week|date=2016-01-29|access-date=2016-02-04|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160204040722/http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/warriors-name-2016-nines-squad/|archive-date=2016-02-04}}</ref> In Round 7 against the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]], Fusitu’a made his return to the Warriors first grade team for the first time since Round 19 of the [[2015 NRL season|2015 season]], playing on the wing and scoring a try in the 24–20 win at [[Westpac Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|author=AAP|url=http://wwos.ninemsn.com.au/2016/04/16/19/56/warriors-hang-on-for-gritty-win|title=Warriors hang on for gritty win - NRL|publisher=Wwos.ninemsn.com.au|date=2016-04-16|access-date=2016-10-08|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160621050550/http://wwos.ninemsn.com.au/2016/04/16/19/56/warriors-hang-on-for-gritty-win|archive-date=2016-06-21|url-status=dead}}</ref> On 7 May 2016, Fusitu'a made his International debut for [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tonga]] against [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]] in the 2016 [[Polynesian Cup]] where he played at fullback in the 18–6 loss at [[Parramatta Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/samoa-holds-on-in-pulsating-pacific-test/tabid/10874/newsid/95681/default.aspx|title=Samoa holds on in pulsating Pacific Test|date=7 May 2016|publisher=nrl.com|access-date=7 May 2016}}</ref> In Round 14, Fusitu'a scored a career high 4 tries against the [[Newcastle Knights]] in their 50–14 win in [[Hunter Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.heraldsun.com.au/sport/nrl/teams/warriors/winger-david-fusitua-shines-as-warriors-thrash-the-disappointing-knights/news-story/107dfa69b96e7049cd9029a1cc87a434|title=Winger David Fusitua shines as Warriors thrash the disappointing Knights|publisher=Herald Sun|access-date=2016-10-08}}</ref>

Fusitu’a finished the [[2016 NRL season]] with him playing in 18 matches and scoring 11 tries for the Warriors. On 4 October 2016, Fusitu’a was selected in the [[New Zealand national rugby league team]] 24-man squad for the [[2016 Rugby League Four Nations|2016 Four Nations]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.newshub.co.nz/home/sport/2016/10/kiwis-name-squad-for-four-nations.html |title=Kiwis name squad for Four Nations |publisher=[[Newshub]] |date=4 October 2016 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180616053408/https://www.newshub.co.nz/home/sport/2016/10/kiwis-name-squad-for-four-nations.html |archive-date=16 June 2018}}</ref> On 6 October 2016, Fusitu'a extended his contract with the Warriors to the end of the 2019 season.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.stuff.co.nz/sport/league/85094449/New-Kiwi-David-Fusitu-a-re-signs-with-Warriors-until-2019|title = New Kiwi David Fusitu'a re-signs with Warriors until 2019|date = 7 October 2016}}</ref> On 11 November 2016, Fusitu’a made his international debut for [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] against [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]], playing on the wing and scoring 2 tries in the shock 18-18 all draw at [[Derwent Park]].<ref>{{Cite news|url=http://www.news.com.au/sport/nrl/scotland-managed-to-draw-with-new-zealand/news-story/c53915e3aa9d14e0b4f37de90173c84a|title = 'One of sport's greatest shocks'|newspaper = News.com.au|date = 12 November 2016}}</ref> On 20 November 2016, Fusitu’a would make his second appearance the Kiwis, playing in the 2016 Four Nations Final against [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]] on the wing in the 34–8 loss at [[Anfield]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/four-nations-final-australia-hammer-new-zealand-348-20161121-gstkra.html|title=Four Nations final: Australia hammer New Zealand 34-8|date=20 November 2016}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2016/11/21/four-nations-final-kiwis-player-ratings/|title = Four Nations Final: Kiwis player ratings|date = 20 November 2016}}</ref>

===2017===
In Round 1, against the [[Newcastle Knights]], Fusitu’a scored his first career hattrick of tries in the 26–22 win at [[Mt Smart Stadium]].<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/david-fusitua-scores-hattrick-as-warriors-down-knights-2622/news-story/eba299abf10c8dea327321ff75ac7949|title=Warriors survive huge Knights scare|newspaper=Daily Telegraph|date=5 March 2017|last1=Toohey|first1=Barry}}</ref> Fusitu’a finished the [[2017 NRL season]] with him playing in all of the Warriors 24 matches and scoring 12 tries. On 4 October, Fusitu’a was selected in the 24-man squad for [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tonga]] for the [[2017 Rugby League World Cup]] after defecting from New Zealand.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.stuff.co.nz/sport/league/97582100/Tonga-names-powerful-squad-packed-with-ex-Kiwis|title=Tonga names powerful squad, packed with ex-Kiwis|date=5 October 2017}}</ref> On 11 November, in the highly entertaining clash against New Zealand, he scored a hattrick of tries as Tonga pulled off the biggest upset of the tournament, defeating the Kiwis 28–22 at [[Waikato Stadium]].<ref>{{Cite news|url=http://www.news.com.au/sport/nrl/rugby-league-world-cup-new-zealand-tonga-war-dances-were-spinetingling/news-story/9e2b6c839588fcaaa632b9e16d1da199|title=Bad blood boils over in war cry|newspaper=News.com.au|date=11 November 2017}}</ref> In the next match against [[Lebanon national rugby league team|Lebanon]], Fusitu’a scored a double in the nail biting 24–22 victory at [[Lancaster Park]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/live-rugby-legaue-world-cup-2017-blog-tonga-v-lebanon-quarter-final/news-story/4d969c6d61a4d8327e7b0c0de876681f|title=Lebanon give Tonga huge fright in sensational quarter final as Mate Ma'a advance|date=18 November 2017}}</ref> Fusitu’a played in 4 matches and scored 5 tries for Tonga in their outstanding tournament.

===2018===
Following a breakout performance for Tonga in the World Cup, in 2018 Fusitu'a produced the finest NRL season of his career to date with the Warriors.  He appeared in 23 games and scored 23 tries, (including two hat-tricks and five doubles), finishing as the NRL's top tryscorer for 2018.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2018/10/19/all-of-david-fusituas-2018-tries/|title=All David Fusitu'a's 2018 tries|date=18 December 2018 }}</ref>  The Warriors finished in the top 8 for the first time since 2011, with Fusitu'a scoring a try in their qualifying final loss to [[Penrith Panthers|Penrith]]. In the annual NRL.com Player's Poll, he earnt 21% of the Best Winger votes, the second highest behind [[Josh Addo-Carr]] who polled 27%.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2018/08/03/nrl.com-players-poll-part-2---best-player-winger-centre-forwards/ |title=NRL.com Players' Poll: Part 2 - Best player, winger, centre, forwards |publisher=NRL |date=5 August 2018 |access-date=21 December 2021}}</ref>  His meteoric rise was cemented in August when the Warriors signed him to a long-term deal, ensuring he would stay with the club until the end of 2023.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.warriors.kiwi/news/2018/08/09/vodafone-warriors-secure-fusitua-until-2023/|title = Vodafone Warriors secure Fusitu'a until 2023|date = 9 August 2018}}</ref>

He was named on the wing again for [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tonga]] in their historic first-ever Test match with the [[Australian Kangaroos]] on 20 October 2018, played in front of a sold-out crowd at [[Mount Smart Stadium]], Auckland.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.radionz.co.nz/news/sport/369091/kangaroos-withstand-spirited-tongan-challenge|title = Kangaroos withstand spirited Tongan challenge|date = 20 October 2018}}</ref>

===2019 & 2020===
In the [[2019 NRL season]], he made 16 appearances and scored five tries as the club missed out on the finals.  In the [[2020 NRL season]], Fusitu'a was limited to only seven appearances as the [[New Zealand Warriors]] again missed out on the finals.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2020/12/01/for--against-warriors-to-make-finals-in-2021/|title=Warriors to make finals in 2021|website=www.nrl.com|date=December 2020}}</ref>

===2021===
On 18 October, he signed a two-year deal with English side [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] despite having two years remaining on his contract with the [[New Zealand Warriors]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.stuff.co.nz/sport/league/warriors/126717292/nrl-leeds-rhinos-confirm-signing-of-former-warriors-winger-david-fusitua/|title=Leeds Rhinos confirm signing of former Warriors winger David Fusitu'a|website=www.stuff.co.nz|date=18 October 2021}}</ref>

===2022===
In round 1 of the 2022 Super League season, he made his club debut for Leeds in their 22-20 loss against [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]]. Fusitu'a missed the clubs 2022 Super League Grand Final loss to [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] due to injury.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.therhinos.co.uk/report/leeds-rhinos-20-warrington-wolves-22/|title=LEEDS RHINOS 20 WARRINGTON WOLVES 22|website=www.therhinos.co.uk}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/leeds-rhinos-news-huge-blow-as-two-more-players-ruled-out-of-grand-final-3847347/|title=Leeds Rhinos news: Huge blow as TWO more players ruled out of Grand Final|website=www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk}}</ref>

===2023===
In round 16 of the 2023 Super League season, Fusitu'a scored two tries as Leeds defeated Huddersfield 54-0.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/65974502|title=Super League: Leeds Rhinos 54-0 Huddersfield Giants - Hosts pile misery on Ian Watson's side|work=BBC Sport }}</ref>
He played a total of 17 games for Leeds in the 2023 Super League season and scored ten tries as the club finished 8th on the table and missed the playoffs.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/leeds-rhinos-2023-season-review-picking-out-the-best-and-worst-players-performances-results-4355920/|title=Leeds Rhinos' 2023 season review: picking out the best and worst players, performances, results|website=www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk}}</ref>

===2024===
Fusitu'a played 12 games for Leeds in the 2024 Super League season as the club finished 8th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/from-310-to-810-peter-smiths-leeds-rhinos-player-ratings-for-2024-season-4799226/|title=Leeds Rhinos player ratings from the 2024 Super League season|website=www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk}}</ref>
